data.title: Samsung Electronics to Buy Back Shares Worth Over KRW3 Trillion
data.description:
Shares of Samsung Electronics rose more than 3% at market close on Wednesday after it announced on Tuesday that it plans to retire 3.048 trillion won worth of treasury stocks under its shares repurchase plan to boost shareholder value.

The shares are expected to be retired on Thursday, Feb. 20, Samsung said in a corporate filing with Korea Exchange.

The board-approved cancellation of treasury shares, acquired on Nov. 15, 2024, will be executed using distributable profits, reducing share count without impacting capital. The process follows a board resolution and does not affect the company's capital structure.

data.title: Delek US Holdings Keeps Quarterly Dividend of $0.255 per Share, Payable on March 10 to Shareholders as of Record on March 3
News Highlights: Top Energy News of the Day - Wednesday at 4 AM ET
data.description:
Occidental Stock Drops After Mixed Results. What's Dragging on Buffett's Favorite.

OXY stock has lost 19% over the past year. Weak crude prices are to blame.

Oil prices end higher after drone attack on Russian pipeline

Oil futures were lifted Tuesday, ending with gains after a drone attack by Ukraine on a pipeline carrying crude from Kazakhstan across southern Russia curtailed flows.

Diamondback Energy to Acquire Double Eagle

The oil and natural gas company reached an agreement with Double Eagle in exchange for 6.9 million shares of its common stock and $3 billion in cash.

Galp Misses Expectations; Launches Buyback

The oil-and-gas producer launched a $262 million share buyback and said its results reflected a strong performance despite a volatile economic environment.

Energy & Utilities Roundup: Market Talk

Gain insight on Repsol, SK Innovation, Seatrium, and more in the latest Market Talks covering the Energy and Utilities sectors.

New Sanctions for Putin's 'Shadow Fleet' Are on the Horizon

The EU is ready to roll out new sanctions on vessels transporting illicit oil, but the Trump administration has yet to say if it will participate.

Clean-Energy Program Fueled by Biden Takes Deep Staff Cuts Under Trump

The number of employees in the Energy Department's loan office has been sliced by roughly 25%.

Eneos Plans to Spin off Metals Subsidiary

The energy company plans to spin off its metals subsidiary and raise as much as $3 billion through a listing that would be one of Japan's biggest such offerings in recent years.

Trump Anxiety Could Boost Sales at These Renewables Companies

The rush to purchase renewable energy equipment before clean energy laws get changed could juice the results of companies that make that equipment.

IEA Slightly Lifts Oil Demand Outlook, Says Supply Surplus Narrowing

The International Energy Agency modestly raised its forecast for global oil-demand growth and said improved compliance with output quotas among members of the OPEC+ alliance is reducing a projected supply surplus in the market.

data.title: Higher Labor, Energy Costs Could Drive U.K. Inflation to Around 4% — Market Talk
data.description: An increase in employers' national insurance contributions and higher energy costs are expected to push up U.K. inflation to around 4% in September, Berenberg economist Andrew Wishart says in a note. The latest data show annual CPI inflation accelerated to 3.0% in January from 2.5% in December. Berenberg expects that higher U.K. inflation will likely cause the Bank of England to keep interest rates on hold after one final 25 basis-point interest-rate cut in May. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: China Subsidies Likely to Boost Domestic Auto Retail Sales — Market Talk
data.description: China's domestic retail sales of cars are expected to rise by 3% in 2025 compared to the previous year, according to Goldman Sachs analysts in a note. The country's passenger vehicle industry saw significantly stronger retail volumes in the 4Q 2024, thanks to expanded trade-in subsidies introduced at the end of July, they note. GS estimates that a total of 1.7 million vehicle sales were driven by the government subsidy last year. With government stimulus for autos extended into this year, GS raises its China passenger vehicle retail forecast for 2025 to 23.6 million from 22.1 million. Sales of new energy vehicles, including both electric vehicles and hybrids, are expected to capture an even larger market share this year, due to trade-in subsidies, strong model launches and price reductions, GS says. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: BAE Systems Posts Double-Digit Jump in Sales as Defense Budgets Climb — Update
data.description:
By Cristina Gallardo

BAE Systems posted double-digit sales growth last year, boosted by increased defense budgets in its main markets due to Russia's war in Ukraine.

The London-listed aerospace and defense manufacturer said Wednesday that sales in 2024 rose by 14% on a constant-currency basis to 28.335 billion pounds, equivalent to $35.74 billion.

The FTSE 100 group, which makes a range of military equipment and provides cybersecurity services, said its order backlog reached a record 77.8 billion pounds at the end of 2024, beating a Visible Alpha estimate of 74.45 billion pounds. The company's order intake came to 33.7 billion pounds.

Orders were boosted by long-term programs in the U.K. and the U.S., warship contracts with Australia, increased munitions demand, and fresh orders for Eurofighter Typhoon fighter jets from Italy and Spain.

Underlying earnings before interest and taxes--the group's preferred measure of profitability--rose to 3.015 billion pounds from 2.68 billion pounds.

All business areas contributed to the results, with electronic systems and platforms and services units performing particularly well, BAE Systems said.

Earnings were further boosted by Colorado-based Ball Aerospace, a supplier of satellite systems, antenna arrays and other location products bought by BAE Systems in a bid to deepen its relationship with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. Its integration into the group's U.S.-based Space and Mission Systems business is going well and enabling cost synergies at SMS, BAE Systems said.

"We remain confident in the positive momentum of our business into the future," Chief Executive Charles Woodburn said, citing the company's record order backlog.

The British company is waiting for clarity on the North Atlantic Treaty Organization's new defense-spending target, which allies are expected to set at the group's annual summit in The Hague in late June, Woodburn told investors during a call Wednesday. NATO's Secretary General Mark Rutte suggested last week that the target should rise from the current 2% of members' gross domestic product to "considerably" higher than 3% in order to fill holes in the alliance's military preparedness and to help deter Russia from any potential attacks in the future.

Woodburn said BAE Systems needs clarity on NATO's medium and long-term military requirements in order to plan production accordingly.

As European governments grapple with the need to consolidate the number of military platforms they use and increase weapons productions across the continent, Woodburn suggested that MBDA--a joint venture between Airbus, BAE Systems and Leonardo to produce missiles--could serve as a potential blueprint for more pan-European collaborations.

The group is also ramping up ammunition production, with new factories coming into operation to meet higher demand for artillery shells across Europe. It hopes in the coming months to increase ammunition production eightfold compared with levels before Russia's large-scale invasion of Ukraine in February 2022, Woodburn said.

For 2025, BAE Systems guided for sales growth of up to 9% and organic growth of 6%-8%, while earnings should rise by between 8% and 10%, it said.

Matt Dorset, equity analyst at Quilter Cheviot, said the company has substantial exposure to European demand, just as the continent, including the U.K., prepares to raise defense spending. "We continue to see structural tailwinds supporting BAE, driven by increased global defense spending," he said.

BAE Systems' board declared a dividend of 20.6 pence, taking the total dividend to 33 pence a share, up 10% on year.

In morning trading, London-listed shares were down 2.1% at 13.08 pounds. The stock has risen 5.2% in the last 12 months.

Write to Cristina Gallardo at cristina.gallardo@wsj.com

data.title: Investors who lost money on Canopy Growth Corporation should contact Levi & Korsinsky about an ongoing investigation - CGC
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / February 19, 2025 / Levi & Korsinsky notifies investors that it has commenced an investigation of Canopy Growth Corporation ("Canopy Growth") concerning possible violations of federal securities laws.

On February 7, 2025, Canopy issued a press release on the Company's financial results for the third quarter of its fiscal year 2025. Canopy reported year-over-year declines in both net revenue and gross margin, contributing to a wider-than-anticipated net loss for the quarter.

Following this news, Canopy's stock price fell 27.34%. To obtain additional information, go to:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/canopy-growth-lawsuit-submission-form-2?prid=130454&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. For seven years in a row, Levi & Korsinsky has ranked in ISS Securities Class Action Services' Top 50 Report as one of the top securities litigation firms in the United States. Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: Euro Area Current Account Surplus Widens in December
data.description:
The current account surplus in the Euro Area widened to €50.5 billion in December 2024 from €39.7 billion a year earlier.

The goods surplus rose to €35.6 billion from €30.7 billion, the services surplus increased to €12.5 billion from €6.7 billion, and the primary income surplus went up to €19.4 billion from €16.9 billion.

Meanwhile, the secondary income gap widened to €17 billion from €14.5 billion.

On a seasonally adjusted basis, the current account surplus rose to €38.4 billion in December from €25 billion in the previous month, above market expectations of €30.2 billion.

data.title: Hung Hing Printing Sees 2024 Loss HK$43M >0450.HK
Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0908 GMT - Oil prices rise in early trading amid market concerns over supply disruptions. Brent crude is up 0.8% at $76.46 a barrel, while WTI trades 0.9% higher to $72.45 a barrel. Gains are largely driven by fears over Kazakhstan's oil flows following a drone attack against a major pumping station in southern Russia. Meanwhile, the Group of Seven is reportedly considering tightening the current oil price cap of $60/bbl on Russia in an effort to cut its revenues. Traders are also waiting to see whether OPEC and its allies will stick to their current output plans or delay them once again. "A delay could wipe out the surplus we expect for the market this year, which would leave prices better supported," ING analysts say. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0857 GMT - BAE Systems released a strong set of 2024 results, Berenberg says in a note. The highlight is free cash flow outperformance of around 900 million pounds. That stems from high cash conversion in the Platforms & Services and Air divisions, Berenberg says. The company's 2025 free cash flow guidance is around 700 million pounds below consensus, although the 2024-25 cumulative free cash flow is around 200 million pounds ahead following the 2024 performance. BAE's share price falls 2.1% in early morning trade but Berenberg says: "This morning's results should be taken positively, in our view." (Alistair.Macdonald@wsj.com)

0853 GMT - Retail sales volume of Chinese automobiles is expected to drop by 5% in 2025 compared to the previous year, according to Bernstein analysts in a note. They attribute this to demand being brought forward by China's revamped trade-in policy and local government subsidies. China faces a challenging macroeconomic outlook and weak consumer confidence, the analysts also note, adding that exports of Chinese cars, however, are expected to remain a growth driver. With relatively lean inventory levels, industry wholesale volumes may grow by 1%, reaching around 27.5 million units this year, Bernstein adds. The brokerage maintains a cautious outlook on China's auto sector and names BYD and Li Auto as their most preferred within the sector, with outperform ratings. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0850 GMT - Baidu's search market share could be threatened after Tencent's Weixin began tests to integrate DeepSeek's model, Daiwa analyst John Choi says in a research note. In January, 22% of Baidu's search queries were AI-powered but not monetized, the analyst notes. Although Baidu has followed suit to integrate DeepSeek into its search engine, Choi thinks the high-quality information from companies' and institutions' official accounts on Tencent's Weixin platform could attract more users in the long term, he says. Meanwhile, Baidu's move to make its next-generation AI model Ernie 4.5 open source amid fierce competition could weigh on its operating margin, Choi says. Daiwa lowers Baidu's rating to outperform from buy and cuts its target to HK$100.00 from HK$108.00. Shares last ended 2.05% lower at HK$88.35. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0843 GMT - Shares in easyJet and TUI fall, dragged by leisure-travel group peer Jet2's warning that its profit margin could come under pressure. easyJet shares in London trade down 2.9% at 502.60 pence while shares for German travel company TUI are 4% lower at 6.81 euros. Jet2 shares are down 9.65% at 14.14 pounds. Jet2 said that although bookings are up on-year for April, May and June, current macro-economic conditions, pressure on consumer incomes and customers' preference for booking late risk weighing on its performance. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0839 GMT - Hong Kong shares closed slightly lower, with the Hang Seng Index dropping 0.1% to 22944.24. Banks led declines on the index. Hang Seng Bank fell 3.9% after reporting 2024 earnings results and Industrial and Commercial Bank of China was off 1.6%. Chip stocks led gains, with sentiment supported by strong AI demand. Hua Hong Semiconductor jumped 23% and SMIC gained 8.4%. The Hang Seng Tech Index rose 0.6% to 5672.24. China Mengniu Dairy gained 10% after saying it doesn't expect impairment losses to affect its dividend. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0829 GMT - European stocks open mixed, with Germany's DAX rising from its latest record closing high and other bourses falling, after U.S. President Trump said he was considering tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceutical products. The Stoxx Europe 600 is down 0.1%, having closed at a record high on Tuesday. Meanwhile, the DAX climbs 0.3% from a record close. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 and France's CAC 40 drop 0.5% and 0.1%, respectively. Italy's FTSE MIB and Spain's Ibex 35--which rose to multiyear highs this week--are up 0.5% and down 0.1%, respectively. There are now many tariff spinning plates in play, but it remains to be seen which of the floated levies will be implemented, strategists at Deutsche bank say in a note. (adria.calatayud@wsj.com)

0828 GMT - Glencore switching its listing to the U.S. would be a positive catalyst for its shares, Jefferies analysts Christopher LaFemina and Patricia Hove write. The Anglo-Swiss miner said Wednesday that it is reviewing its primary-listing status. Glencore's $1 billion share buyback also helps ease concerns over the slight miss to 2024 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization expectations, they write. Meanwhile, Glencore is not expected to benefit from strong commodity tailwinds until next year, they write. Shares trade down 3% at 342.7 pence. (adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

0824 GMT - MTU Aero Engines raised its outlook after delivering earnings which beat expectations, JPMorgan analysts David H. Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say in a research note. However, the company's cash generation was weaker than expected, which may temper the share price reaction, they say. Fourth-quarter cash generation was 75 million euros below views due to higher-than-expected recall costs on the GTF engine and supply chain challenges, the analysts say. Consensus for cash generation could be trimmed as a result, though earnings consensus for 2025 could rise by a few percentage points, they note. Shares trade 4.7% lower to 330 euros. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

0823 GMT - BAE Systems reported sales growth across all five segments, with the electronic systems and platforms-and-services units performing particularly well, Matt Dorset, equity analyst at Quilter Cheviot, says. The demand for BAE's products and services is evident, with the order backlog reaching a record high of nearly 80 billion pounds--almost three times its annual sales, he says. The British aerospace and defense manufacturer has substantial exposure to European demand, just as the continent, including the U.K., prepares to step up defense spending, Dorset says. "We continue to see structural tailwinds supporting BAE, driven by increased global defense spending," he writes. Shares are up 0.5% at 13.44 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0810 GMT - ASR Nederland didn't provide an update on the almost 30% stake that is owned by Aegon and resulted from ASR's purchase of its peer's Dutch insurance business in 2023 but posted solid results, J.P. Morgan says in a research note after the Dutch insurer's annual print. "We would view any move by Aegon to start reducing its stake, accompanied by additional neutralising buybacks from ASR, as potential positive catalyst--but the timing remains uncertain," they say. The shares should react positively to the solid set of results and lift consensus estimates, analysts write. ASR rises 3.75% to 50.58 euros while Aegon is up 1.2% at 6.48 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0805 GMT - Singapore's grocers and malls are set to be the biggest beneficiaries of generous handouts from the country's budget, say DBS Group Research analysts in a note. A set of support measures was announced to help households and individuals tackle rising cost-of-living pressures. Various vouchers will support retail-related spending, particularly on essentials at suburban retail malls, DBS says. Among other beneficiaries are supermarkets and food-court operators, the bank adds. Sheng Siong Group is best positioned to gain as a pure domestic grocery retailer, DBS says. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: China Southern Airlines Appoints Executive Vice President
data.description:
China Southern Airlines approved the appointment of Zeng Yong Chao as its executive vice president on Tuesday, a same-day bourse filing said.

The board also approved the appointments of Chen Zhe as the chief pilot, and Cai Qi as the chief security officer on the same day.

The appointments approval came as Wu Ying Xiang and Wu Rong Xin ceased to be the executive vice presidents due to work changes.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks End Flat on Looming Tariff Fears; Tech Stocks Slip
data.description:
Hong Kong stocks fell marginally on Wednesday as investor sentiment remained on edge amid news of expanding tariffs by the US to be announced by April 2.

The Hang Seng Index fell slightly to finish the day at 22,944.24. The Hang Seng China Enterprises Index also marginally decreased to end at 8,463.10.

Investors remained cautious as fears of a trade war with the US resurfaced after President Donald Trump said Tuesday he is expected to apply duties of around 25% on the imports of automobiles, semiconductors, and pharmaceuticals, Bloomberg reported Wednesday.

Trump added that the levies will rise substantially over the course of a year, according to the report.

The US government previously announced 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum products which will become effective in March.

In corporate news, shares of multiple tech heavyweights slumped Wednesday, with Meituan falling 3% and Alibaba Group and Baidu each logging a 2% drop, respectively.

data.title: BP Investor Group Demands Shareholder Vote on Scaling Back Climate Targets
data.description:
BP (BP.L) is facing a call from 48 institutional investors to hold a shareholder vote regarding any plans to scale back climate goals, London's Financial Times reported on Wednesday.

The demand follows a recent push by activist investor Elliott Management to sell significant assets, including green energy investments, the report noted.

The investors, including Rathbones Investment Management, Phoenix Group, Robeco, and Royal London Asset Management hold a combined stake of 2.5%, according to the report. Elliott owns an almost 5% shareholding in BP.

BP confirmed receiving the letter from investors when reached by MT Newswires.

data.title: Malaysia Shares Edge Lower Amid Investor Caution — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index closed 0.2% lower at 1580.88. Stock trading was cautious, with a downside bias, pressured by continued selling in rubber-glove shares, TA Securities' Stephen Soo writes in a note. A weak earnings outlook also weighed on investors' sentiment, the analyst adds. Soo says the KLCI could find support at 1550 and face resistance at 1605. Among decliners, Mr. D.I.Y. Group was 6.7% lower and PPB Group lost 5.5%. Meanwhile, Petronas Dagangan was 4.0% higher and RHB Bank added 2.0%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: India's Drone Destination gains on 13 mln-rupee contract
data.description:
** Drone Destination rises 5% to 119 rupees; set to snap eight-day losing streak

** Co gets contract worth 13.4 million rupees ($154,333) for aerial survey and data processing from GarudaUAV Soft Solutions

** More than 81,500 shares traded, 3x the 30-day avg

** Stock down 42% YTD

($1 = 86.8250 Indian rupees)

data.title: Landai Technology Group Establishes Singapore Subsidiary to Facilitate Production Base in Thailand
data.description:
Landai Technology Group established an indirect subsidiary in Singapore, Tephris Tech, as part of a strategic plan to invest in a production base in Thailand, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

Tephris Tech, which is fully owned by company subsidiary Zyphra Tech, was registered on Feb. 17, with a capital of $100,000.

This move is part of the company's broader plan to invest up to 200 million yuan in Thailand for land acquisition, construction and other related activities.

Landai Technology Group manufactures passenger car transmission gear and shell parts. Its shares closed more than 5% higher Wednesday.

data.title: Piramal Pharma Receives Six Observations from US FDA After Turbhe, India Unit Inspection
data.description:
Piramal Pharma has received six observations in the Form-483 issued by the US Food and Drug Administration after the inspection of its Turbhe facility in India, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

The US FDA conducted general good manufacturing practices inspection at the Turbhe facility from Feb. 11 to Feb. 17.

"Observations are largely around improvement of procedures and practices and not related to data integrity," the company said, adding it was preparing a detailed response to the observations.

Shares of the company were up 1% in recent trade.

data.title: Bic rises on strong Q4 revenue and faster-than-expected margin gains
data.description:
** Shares in the French pencil manufacturer Bic rise 3%, pares gains after rising as much as 5.9%, after the company reported a strong revenue for Q4 2024 and guided for further growth in 2025

** The company announced a revenue of EUR 517.5 million ($540.06 million)and said it expects 2025 net sales growth of between +4% and +6%

** An analyst contacted by Reuters cited the company's Q4 EBIT margin as the main highlight of their result, higher than expected at 6.5%, one year ahead of their plan

** Up to the previous session's close, shares were up 3.1%​ YTD

($1 = 0.9582 euros)

data.title: Fiverr International's Q4 Adjusted Earnings, Revenue Rise; Provides Q1, FY2025 Guidance
data.description:
Fiverr International reported Q4 adjusted earnings Wednesday of $0.64 per diluted share, up from $0.56 a year earlier.

Analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.68.

Revenue for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $103.7 million, compared with $91.5 million a year earlier.

Analysts surveyed by FactSet expected $101.4 million.

For Q1, the company said it expects revenue to be between $103.5 million and $108.5 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $102.3 million.

The company expects its 2025 revenue to be $422 million to $438 million. Analysts polled by FactSet expect $418.7 million.

data.title: Aldar Properties JV Unveils Residential Development in UAE
data.description:
Aldar Properties (ADX:ALDAR) and Dubai Holding launched a residential complex, The Wilds, in Dubai, United Arab Emirates.

The real estate developer's project, which features 1,700 homes, will start selling on Feb. 27, according to a Wednesday release.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: BP Reviews Options for Lubricants Business Including Sale
data.description:
(Updates to add BP's statement)

BP (BP.L) is considering various options for its lubricants business Castrol, including a potential divestment, Bloomberg News reported Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The unnamed sources said the business could be worth $10 billion, adding that the sale is being considered to appease investors after years of underperformance, with a potential announcement of the plan expected during the British oil and gas company's capital markets day on Feb. 26.

The people added that activist investor Elliott Investment Management identified the business as one of the assets that BP could sell. Deliberations are ongoing and a final decision is yet to be made, the source noted.

BP told MT Newswires it does not comment on market rumors and speculation.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: India's Cello World falls after fire at unit
data.description:
** Cello World falls 1.6% to 604.50 rupees

** Homeware products maker says furnace at its subsidiary's Opal division caught fire, but was quickly contained

** No casualties or injuries reported so far

** Co's unit ascertaining losses in fire

** CELO fell 3.9% in 2024

data.title: Eurozone December current account balance €50.5 billion vs €34.6 billion prior
data.description:
Key points:
  • Prior €34.6 billion

Slight delay in the release by the source. The seasonally adjusted figure is €38.4 billion for the month of December, bringing the cumulative total for the year 2024 to €419 billion. That is a step up relative to the €241 billion total seen in 2023 as trade conditions in particular are normalising after the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

data.title: Capital Southwest Makes Executive Changes
data.description:
Capital Southwest said late Tuesday it has appointed chief financial officer Michael Sarner as president and chief executive officer succeeding Bowen Diehl, effective Feb. 17.

Diehl will remain with the company in an advisory role for at least one year.

Meanwhile, Chris Rehberger will assume the role of chief financial officer, and Tabitha Geiger will assume the role of chief compliance officer, the company said.
